boe_references.title: The Supervisory Statement (SS) shown on this page is effective from 28/07/2016. Past versions of this SS can be found here.
CP42/15 - Capital extractions by run-off firms within the general insurance sector

paragraphs.summary: The purpose of this statement is to set out the Prudential Regulation Authority’s (PRA’s) expectation of compliance with existing prudential provisions within the PRA Rulebook for run-off firms in the

This statement is aimed at general insurance firms in run-off and highlights some factors that the PRA expects the senior management of a run-off firm to take into account when considering making a re

Insurers must comply with relevant provisions in the PRA Rulebook including the Risk Management chapter of the Conditions Governing Business Part of the PRA Rulebook, which includes requirements to un
This supervisory statement provides additional clarification of the PRA’s expectations of firms in respect of these existing requirements. Its purpose is to explain the PRA’s expectations that:

This statement expands on the PRA’s general approach as set out in its Approach Document,2 and is designed to help ensure the PRA meets its statutory objectives of ensuring safety and soundness of the
General insurance firms in run-off occasionally approach the PRA with requests to extract capital. The PRA recognises that this may be a legitimate request in certain circumstances, for example where

However, capital extractions during the life of a run-off inevitably weaken the level of protection available for remaining policyholders. This is of particular concern for the PRA in respect of firms

sections.chapter_title: PRA expectations of run-off firms proposing capital extractions

paragraphs.summary: The PRA expects senior management and boards of run-off firms to assess carefully the level of capital required on an ongoing basis to ensure that the firm can run-off their business in an orderly fas
Where a run-off firm wishes to undertake a capital extraction, the PRA expects the firm first to take the following steps:

The PRA expects firms to request its views at an early stage and ensure that any concerns it may have in relation to a proposed capital extraction are properly addressed before the proposal is impleme

Alongside its request, the firm should provide the PRA with a copy of the analysis submitted to its board showing the projected evolution of the financial resources and capital needs and requirements,

Where a firm has commissioned any independent actuarial review of its analysis, or other relevant factors such as an analysis of the adequacy of its policy records, it should make the PRA aware of thi

The PRA expects Solvency II firms to seek to hold capital to manage and mitigate the risks identified in the ORSA and capital extractions should be considered accordingly. The PRA will not expect Solv
